author: Sangharakshita
title: Contents of Men-s Order Convention 1985 - Questions and Answers
content:
SANGHARAKSHITA IN SEMINAR
QUESTION AND ANSWER SESSIONS
MEN'S ORDER CONVENTION 1985
CONTENTS



2-4 What is Vedanta? The vedas
4 What did vedanta absorb from Buddhism?
6 Comparing Buddhist and Hindu visualisation and m antra recitation
Bodhisattvas are a manifestation of sunyata
Sunyata is not
an ontological principle
Chastity - of body, speech and mind
6 The historical importance of chastity in Buddhis m
7 The modern devaluation of friendship and overemp hasis on sex
Working towards a higher degree of chastity from wh erever one is
10 The historicity of Sakyamuni, Gautama, the Budd ha
Why historicity is questioned
11 Healthy and unhealthy competition
12 The history of mantra chanting in the FWBO The Padmasambhava mantra
13 The Tara mantra
13 The possibility of structured training in visua lisation
14 Meditation posture Meditation cushions
15 The Buddha's assenting by silence Bhante's not assenting by silence
16 Visualisation, especially of Vajrasattva, as a cure for serious illness?
16 Dharmachari Mahadhammavira - reflections on the dead Order member from
India's happiness and goodwill
19 Suicide and Buddhism
21 Next Session Using English literature to possibly enhance one's experience of the Dharma
22 Music in Sufism and in Buddhism Using music in small quantities to contact positive emotions
23 Could the development of a musical tradition in the FWBO be encouraged?
24 "How does friendship relate with Stream Entry?"
24 The first fetter - habit - sakayadrsti
25 Doubt - vicikitsa 'Going through the motions' - silavrataparamarsa
25-26 Visualisation, especially of Vajrasattva, as a cure for serious illness? (the same
question as the previous session with a similar, b ut slightly different, answer from Bhante)
26 Where is Devadatta at the moment and will he ga in enlightenment?!
27 People finding their way back to the Dharma eve n if they have previously
abandoned it What to do with unwanted demons
28 Maintaining one's awareness when travelling on the London Underground
The use of personal stereo systems (Walkmans/men/pe ople) and the ensuing

possible alienation and 'schizophrenia'
29 The expressing of 'negativity' Different types of people having different types of 'negativity' - those who
self-perpetuate it and those who have a finite amou nt which can be exhausted
30 Bhante's contact with Dilgo Khyentse Rimpoche
31 The wearing of the blue shirt in India and else where
Bhante's robes worn when conferring ordinations
33 "There is not a
Sangha in the East at all"
The different nikayas and their Sanghakammas
34 How are beings reborn in the human realm and no t the animal realm?

35 The Samadhinimita - first characteristic of con centration - the four
satipatthanas Things which are spoken or written generally but be ing appropriate to many
individuals
36 Resolution of rifts in the Sangha/Order
38 The difficulties of asking potentially libellou s questions!
39 Some other Buddhist groups not succeeding in co mmunication Dharma

What is Western Buddhism
?
Alcoholism in some Zen groups
40 Alcoholism, sexual promiscuity and authoritaria nism in some traditional
Buddhism groups Buddhist teachers having bodyguards and the Buddha sending bodyguards
away
40-42 Lama Govinda's Aryamaitreyamandala Have other Buddhist groups failed to communicate th e principles of Dharma
effectively?
43 Bhante's own comments to the Order Rifts in the Order
43-44 The need for deepening trust in the Order
44 Why there is the structure of Order/Chapter mee tings, Order Weekends and
Order conventions - to help realise the connection with the greater Order
44 The importance of writing to Shabda, the Order newsletter
45 Encouragement and advice for becoming good publ ic speakers
Becoming effective writers
